heading: Members: effect of time lost
label: 972 Members: effect of time lost

Amendments2004—Subsec. (c). added subsec. (c).Pub. L. 108–3751997—Subsec. (b). substituted “” for “the date of the enactment of the National Defense Authorization Act for Fiscal Year 1996” in introductory provisions.Pub. L. 105–85February 10, 19961996—, substituted “Members: effect of time lost” for “Enlisted members: required to make up time lost” as section catchline.Pub. L. 104–106, § 561(c)(1), designated existing provisions as subsec. (a), inserted heading, added par. (3), redesignated par. (5) as (4), struck out former pars. (3) and (4), and added subsec. (b). Prior to amendment, subsec. (a)(3) and (4) read as follows:Pub. L. 104–106, § 561(a)“(3) is confined for more than one day while awaiting trial and disposition of his case, and whose conviction has become final;“(4) is confined for more than one day under a sentence that has become final; or”.

children.heading: .—Enlisted Members Required To Make Up Time Lost
children.label: a .—Enlisted Members Required To Make Up Time Lost

children.text: is absent from his organization, station, or duty for more than one day without proper authority, as determined by competent authority;
is confined by military or civilian authorities for more than one day in connection with a trial, whether before, during, or after the trial; or

is unable for more than one day, as determined by competent authority, to perform his duties because of intemperate use of drugs or alcoholic liquor, or because of disease or injury resulting from his misconduct;

children.heading: .—Officers Not Allowed Service Credit for Time Lost
children.label: b .—Officers Not Allowed Service Credit for Time Lost

children.text: is absent from his organization, station, or duty for more than one day without proper authority, as determined by competent authority;
is confined by military or civilian authorities for more than one day in connection with a trial, whether before, during, or after the trial; or

is unable for more than one day, as determined by competent authority, to perform his duties because of intemperate use of drugs or alcoholic liquor, or because of disease or injury resulting from his misconduct;

children.heading: .—Waiver of Recoupment of Time Lost for Confinement
children.label: c .—Waiver of Recoupment of Time Lost for Confinement
A
children.text: the charge is dismissed before or during trial in a final disposition of the charge; or

children.label: B
children.text: the trial results in an acquittal of the charge.

children.label: A
children.text: the conviction is set aside in a final disposition of such charge, other than in a grant of clemency; or

children.label: B
children.text: a judgment of acquittal or a dismissal is entered upon a reversal of the conviction on appeal.
